original_reasoning: خوت here is stealthily taking prey, and εἴχετε is you had or held; seizure and having form a plausible action-to-result chain.

Arabic غور = depth, hidden interior, going deep into a thing. Greek allegoria literally 'speaking otherwise/hidden meaning' from allos+agoreuein. The conceptual core 'hidden depth of meaning' aligns directly — Arabic ghawr al-kalam means 'depth of speech', exactly the allegorical dimension. Strong mafahim link.

Arabic shafaza = kicked someone with the front of the foot. Greek sphadazo = struggle convulsively, kick about, writhe. Both denote violent kicking/spasming motion. Phonetic resemblance shafz/sphad is striking. Strong concrete link.
Arabic صفو 'pure, clear, unclouded' vs σαφής 'clear, plain, manifest'. Both center on clarity concept; real semantic overlap

Arabic al-kal includes 'buying or selling a debt you have on one man with a debt another has on him' — debt-trading/accounting. Greek kalkoulator (Latin loan, calculator) = accountant. Direct semantic match in the accounting/debt-management field. Strong link.
Scale or balance can extend to leastness on a measure continuum.

Arabic habr = beauty, fine appearance, also 'rabbi/scholar' (al-habr). Greek cheroubeis = pl of cheroub 'cherub' (Hebrew loan). Both relate to sacred holy figures — Arabic habr (rabbi/scholar) and cherub are religious-sacred terms with Semitic origin. Strong combined link.

Arabic khandaris = old aged date/wine, also 'I think it is foreign-borrowed'. Greek chondrinos = made of groats/coarse meal (from chondros 'groats'). The Arabic word is itself a Greek loan from chondros/chondrinos. Phonetic resemblance is direct. Strong borrowing link.
